subject: Science
chapter_name: Changes Around Us: Physical and Chemical
overall_learning_objectives: Distinguish between physical and chemical changes
Identify examples of changes occurring in daily life
Understand that physical changes are usually reversible
Recognize that chemical changes result in new substances
Observe and describe the process of rusting
Understand the concept of crystallization as a physical change
Learn simple ways to prevent rusting

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Changes
teaching_strategy: Real-life examples (Inflating a balloon, cooking an egg)
student_activities: Make a list of 5 changes you see at home
Fold a paper into a plane and then unfold it (discussion on reversibility)

lesson_plan.title: Physical Changes
topics_covered: Changes in shape, size, state, and color
teaching_strategy: Concept explanation using water cycle examples
student_activities: Dissolve sugar in water and discuss if it's still sugar
Stretch a rubber band and observe the change
assessment: Identify physical changes from a picture set

lesson_plan.title: Chemical Changes
topics_covered: Signs of chemical change (smell, gas, light, heat)
teaching_strategy: Comparing 'Burning a paper' vs. 'Tearing a paper'
student_activities: Observe the smell of spoiled food vs. fresh food
Draw a 'Before and After' diagram of a burning candle
assessment: Quick quiz on signs of chemical changes

lesson_plan.title: Interesting Chemical Reactions
topics_covered: Reaction between Vinegar and Baking Soda (Gas production)
Copper Sulphate and Iron reaction (Color change)
student_activities: Listen for the 'hissing' sound of Vinegar + Baking soda
Note the color change in a copper sulphate solution

lesson_plan.title: Rusting of Iron
topics_covered: Factors needed for rusting: Air (Oxygen) and Water (Moisture)
Why iron objects get damaged over time
teaching_strategy: Simple word equation: $Iron + Oxygen + Water ightarrow Rust$
student_activities: Identify rusted objects in the school playground
Discuss why a bicycle left in the rain gets 'brown spots'
assessment: Oral quiz on factors of rusting
Labeling a diagram of a rusted object

lesson_plan.title: Preventing Rusting & Crystallization
topics_covered: Prevention: Painting, Oiling, and Galvanization (Easy intro)
teaching_strategy: Showing samples of painted vs. unpainted iron
student_activities: Observe the shape of crystals through a magnifying glass
Brainstorm: 'How do we save our gates from rusting?'

lesson_plan.title: Revision and Assessment
teaching_strategy: Flashcard activity: 'Is it Physical or Chemical?'

teacher_tip: Use a 'Magic vs. Science' approach. Tell students that a physical change is like a costume change—the person is the same. A chemical change is like a transformation—something entirely new appears! This helps Class 7 students internalize the core difference easily.
